title: Hierarchical names in genblk may conflict with existing identifiers
body:
I've noticed that yosys generates escaped identifiers for hierarchical names (for example `a` in the following code has a hierarchical name `genblk1.a` and yosys will create an escaped identifier `\genblk1.a `), but if the escaped identifier is already taken, yosys does not check for this conflict and an error is generated.

BTW the same problem exists in `flatten` pass but is handled by creating uniquified names. The same solution can be applied in genblk (such as creating an identifier `\genblk1.a_1`).

I also wonder if `hdlname` attributes can be generated for genblk items (`attribute \hdlname "genblk1 a"` in this case) ?

## Steps to reproduce the issue

Use `read_verilog` to read the following code:

```verilog
module top();
  wire [2:0] \genblk1.a ;
  if (1) wire [1:0] a;
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

Verilog frontend finished successfully. Two wires with width 3 and 2 respectively are generated in intermediate representation.

## Actual behavior

The following error is generated:

```
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: genblk2.v
Parsing Verilog input from `genblk2.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.
genblk2.v:1: ERROR: Incompatible re-declaration of wire \genblk1.a.
```
code_blocks:
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: genblk2.v
Parsing Verilog input from `genblk2.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.
genblk2.v:1: ERROR: Incompatible re-declaration of wire \genblk1.a.
